steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two independent clauses: (1) "Her work challenges conventional notions of race, gender, history, [blank]" and (2) "she is credited with expanding the horizons of conceptual photographic art." Each clause has its own subject and verb and can stand alone. The blank must join them correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘memory, and’: Completes the list (race, gender, history, memory) and then uses ", and" to join the two independent clauses — the correct pattern for a coordinating conjunction between two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘memory and’: Omits the required comma before "and" when joining two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘memory,’: Joins two independent clauses with only a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘memory’: Leaves the two independent clauses fused with no punctuation or connector. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
